$comment: — Performance budgets. Bump baselines deliberately with the regressing PR; never silently. See docs/PERF-BUDGETS.md.

bundleSize.$comment: Per-chunk gzipped size ceiling in KB. Only chunks >= 5 KB gz are tracked here; smaller route splits and micro-icon chunks are noise. Chunk names strip the Rolldown content hash (e.g. SalesPage-Br3xY9Q_.js -> SalesPage).
bundleSize.$baseline: Captured from the Operator Deck Base 11 validation build and the recovery-ownership band on 2026-07-21. The shared index includes the typed control foundation; the design-system and operational-ownership boards stay route-split. Bilingual Operations copy owns an explicit 8 kB ceiling.

trpcLatencyMs.$comment: p95 latency budget in milliseconds for a curated set of tRPC procedures. CI runs warmupIterations warmups then samplesPerProcedure measurements, computes p95, and asserts it falls within budget * (1 + thresholdPercent/100). Bias the procedure list toward routes the operator opens repeatedly during the day.

dataScale.$comment: Operator Deck live data-volume contract. The design-system route renders this many deterministic rows through the production DataTable; Playwright fails when the virtual window mounts more body rows than maxMountedRows.

storeProfile.$comment: Store-sized local SQLite contract. ci:server seeds the deterministic mega preset, proves minimum tenant row volumes, measures hot operational reads through the real tRPC caller, and pins the tenant-scoped indexes selected by SQLite EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN.

operationalProfile.$comment: Operator Deck Base 11. Store-scale write and desktop continuity budgets. Server CI measures the maximum supported launch-product import; desktop CI measures an encrypted backup round trip, built-runtime launch, and bounded database-lifecycle queue.

electronMemoryMb.$comment: — Electron main + renderer working-set ceiling in MB, measured via app.getAppMetrics() by scripts/check-electron-memory.mjs (PUNTOVIVO_MEASURE_MEMORY mode). is warn-first: the gate prints the report but never fails the build (pass --strict / PUNTOVIVO_MEMORY_STRICT=1 to enforce). It also self-skips when Electron cannot launch (no .vite/build bundle / no display), so ci:desktop stays green on ubuntu until the xvfb CI-launch hardening lands. Baselines captured from a real local run; bump deliberately with the regressing PR.
electronMemoryMb.$baseline: — captured locally (macOS, renderer served by dev:web): main ~250-258 MB, renderer ~159-160 MB across runs. Baselines set just above the observed max; the 25% threshold absorbs run-to-run working-set variance in the warn-first phase.

lighthouse.$comment: /  — Lighthouse web-vitals budgets per top user-facing route. ci:web runs scripts/run-lighthouse-gate.mjs, which seeds an isolated demo DB, serves the built web app, and invokes scripts/check-lighthouse.mjs with --strict --require-measurement so missing proof or over-budget metrics fail push-CI. Local operator diagnostics can still run pnpm run perf:lighthouse in warn-first/self-skip mode. lcpMs/ttiMs in ms and cls use thresholdPercent for run variance; score is an exact 0-100 minimum floor.
lighthouse.$baseline: — captured locally (macOS, DEV build served by dev:server + dev:web, demo-co seed, Lighthouse desktop preset with simulated throttling). The script warms each route first (Vite compiles route modules on first hit), then measures warm. These are DEV-build numbers (no minify/treeshake) under Lighthouse's 4x-CPU throttle, NOT production figures — the gate detects regressions from this local baseline. The 30% threshold absorbs Lighthouse run-to-run variance in the warn-first phase. Re-capture deliberately with pnpm run perf:lighthouse and copy the printed measured= values.
